mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates a healthy expansionary environment where corporate revenues grow without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% and CPIAUCSL growth is below 3%, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases during expansion periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.5%, markets anticipate aggressive Federal Reserve rate hikes to combat inflation, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero Rank IC against 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of high-growth payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which expands corporate revenue streams and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined RSAFS and HOUST uptrend fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a negative cumulative alpha over a 2-year out-of-sample period or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a contracting manufacturing sector and weakening labor demand, which reduces earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or the interaction term shows no predictive power (p > 0.05) in regression tests.
